commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Wood. Hebrew hamsuccan, (Haydock) which Septuagint, Chaldean, and St. Jerome explain of a sort of wood used for idols. Moderns take it to be "a rich "or rather "a poor man. He who is mean in his offering, chooses wood that "(Calmet) (Protestants)
